hero.headline: NIU Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Noida International University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with an HRM specialisation. NAAC A+, WES recognised. Total all-in fee Rs 97,600 after 25% Early Bird discount. Training and Development, Talent Management, Performance and Reward Management, HR Analytics, Strategic HRM, and Cross Cultural Management in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: NIU Online MBA HRM: NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, WES recognised, Rs 97,600 all-in after 25% Early Bird. HRM depth with Training and Development, Talent Management, HR Analytics, Strategic HRM, and Leadership Power and Politics. Strong for budget-conscious HR professionals targeting HR manager and talent management careers.

about.body:
The NIU Online MBA in Human Resource Management is offered by Noida International University, Greater Noida. NIU holds NAAC A+ accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. The degree is WES recognised for international credential evaluation.

The HRM specialisation covers 10 dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects spanning both traditional HR functions and modern HR technology. Subjects include Training and Development, Organization Design and Structure, Industrial Relations and Labour Laws, Talent Management, Performance and Reward Management, HR Analytics, Strategic HRM, Cross Cultural and Global Management, Negotiation and Persuasion, and Leadership Power and Politics.

NIU is not NIRF ranked, and its HR alumni network is smaller than established names like Amity or NMIMS. The programme's primary advantage is the Rs 97,600 all-in fee. For working HR executives and talent acquisition professionals in Delhi-NCR who need a credentialed MBA to move into HR manager or HRBP roles, NIU offers wide HRM subject coverage at a fraction of competing programmes.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply strategic frameworks, competitive analysis, and corporate strategy to HR-aligned business planning.

sem3Subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
sem3Subjects.description: Study business law, contract law, company law, and regulatory compliance relevant to HR professionals.

sem3Subjects.name: Training and Development
sem3Subjects.description: Design training needs analysis, learning programme delivery, effectiveness measurement, and L&D strategy for organisations.

sem3Subjects.name: Organization Design and Structure
sem3Subjects.description: Study organisational structures, job design, reporting hierarchies, and restructuring frameworks for HR-led transformation.

sem3Subjects.name: Industrial Relations and Labour Laws
sem3Subjects.description: Study Indian labour law, industrial disputes resolution, trade union management, and compliance with labour codes.

sem3Subjects.name: Talent Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design talent acquisition pipelines, succession planning frameworks, employer branding, and retention strategies.

sem3Subjects.name: Performance and Reward Management
sem3Subjects.description: Implement performance appraisal systems, KPI frameworks, compensation structuring, and incentive programme design.

sem4Subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
sem4Subjects.description: Study venture planning and HR considerations for startups including founding team design and early-stage hiring.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Marketing and E-Commerce
sem4Subjects.description: Apply digital strategies to employer branding, recruitment marketing, and HR service delivery channels.

sem4Subjects.name: HR Analytics
sem4Subjects.description: Use workforce data for attrition prediction, hiring funnel analysis, employee engagement measurement, and HR dashboard design.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic HRM
sem4Subjects.description: Align HR strategy with business objectives, workforce planning, HR transformation, and organisational change management.

sem4Subjects.name: Cross Cultural and Global Management
sem4Subjects.description: Manage diverse and geographically distributed teams, cross-cultural communication, and global HR policy design.

sem4Subjects.name: Negotiation and Persuasion
sem4Subjects.description: Apply negotiation frameworks, conflict resolution techniques, and persuasion strategies for HR business partnering.

sem4Subjects.name: Leadership Power and Politics
sem4Subjects.description: Study leadership theories, organisational politics, power dynamics, and influence strategies for senior HR roles.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all NIU Online MBA specialisations. Strategic Management and Legal Aspects of Business are common Sem 3 subjects. Entrepreneurship Development and Digital Marketing and E-Commerce are common Sem 4 subjects.

whoHires.body:
HRM MBA graduates target these industries and roles:

**Industries:** IT services, BFSI, Manufacturing, FMCG, Consulting, E-commerce, Pharma, Telecom

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Associate, L&D Coordinator, Payroll Executive, Employee Engagement Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** HR Manager, HR Business Partner, Talent Acquisition Manager, L&D Manager, Compensation and Benefits Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** HR Director, VP Human Resources, CHRO, Head of Talent, Head of L&D

**Typical hiring organisations in Delhi-NCR:** Infosys, TCS, Wipro, HCL Technologies, Genpact, Maruti Suzuki, Hindustan Unilever, HDFC Bank, Deloitte India, Accenture India, Flipkart, Zomato

skills.body:
The NIU HRM specialisation builds skills tested by IT services, FMCG, and BFSI HR teams:

**Core HR skills:**
- Talent Management: talent acquisition pipeline design, succession planning, employer branding, and retention programme management for TA and talent management roles
- Training and Development: training needs analysis, L&D programme design, effectiveness measurement, and learning technology for L&D manager roles
- Performance and Reward Management: performance appraisal systems, KPI design, compensation benchmarking, and incentive structures for C&B and HRBP roles
- Industrial Relations and Labour Laws: Indian labour codes, dispute resolution, trade union management, and statutory compliance for IR and compliance roles

**Strategic HR skills:**
- Strategic HRM: HR strategy alignment with business objectives, workforce planning, and organisational transformation for senior HRBP and HR director roles
- HR Analytics: workforce data analysis, attrition modelling, engagement dashboards, and people analytics for HR analytics and HR technology roles

**Leadership skills:**
- Leadership Power and Politics: influence strategies, organisational power dynamics, and leadership development for senior HR and C-suite roles
- Negotiation and Persuasion: conflict resolution, stakeholder management, and business case presentation for HR business partnering

**Soft skills:** Employee communication, change management communication, cross-cultural team management, and executive stakeholder management.

comparisons.body:
Comparing NIU Online MBA HRM against peers:

**vs Galgotias Online MBA (Rs 86,400):** Galgotias is slightly cheaper and NCR-based with a Master Thesis component. NIU HRM offers 10 dedicated HR subjects including HR Analytics and Leadership Power and Politics. Both are NAAC A+ without NIRF ranking.

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA (Rs 94,000-98,000):** Near-identical fee bracket. Both lack NIRF ranking. NIU HRM has strong IR and Labour Laws coverage alongside modern HR Analytics. Choose based on specific subject preferences.

**vs IGNOU MBA (Rs 66,000):** IGNOU is Rs 31,600 cheaper with government university brand. IGNOU HRM has limited HR Analytics and modern HR subject depth. NIU offers broader HR subject coverage with Strategic HRM and Cross Cultural Management.

**vs Amity Online MBA HRM (Rs 2,25,000):** Amity is NIRF #22 with strong pan-India HR alumni network at 2.3x the cost. For large-company HRBP roles where brand matters, Amity justifies the premium. NIU wins on affordability.

**vs CU Online MBA HRM (Rs 1,65,000):** CU is NIRF #19 with PwC partnerships. CU costs Rs 67,400 more. For recruiter brand recognition in HR, CU is the stronger choice.

**vs LPU Online MBA HRM (Rs 1,61,000):** LPU is NIRF #31 with zero-cost EMI. LPU costs Rs 63,400 more. NIU wins on affordability; LPU offers better brand and EMI terms.

fees.body:
The NIU Online MBA in Human Resource Management fee structure:

**After 25% Early Bird Discount (all-in):**
- Tuition: Rs 88,500
- Examination fee: Rs 8,000 (Rs 2,000 per semester x 4)
- Registration fee: Rs 1,100 (one-time, serves as application fee)
- Total all-in cost: Rs 97,600

**Standard fee (before scholarship):**
- Semester fee: Rs 29,500 per semester
- Examination fee: Rs 2,000 per semester
- Registration fee: Rs 1,100 (one-time)

**EMI option:**
EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm the interest rate and EMI terms with the admissions team before committing.

**Scholarships:** No scholarships beyond the 25% Early Bird discount. No bundled perks or certification add-ons.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with NIU each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Noida International University (NIU) and carries the MBA designation. The Human Resource Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is WES recognised for international credential evaluation. It is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from NIU Online MBA HRM students. Ratings from post-completion feedback.

items.name: Neha
items.liked: HR Analytics and Strategic HRM subjects were directly applicable to my HRBP role. The Rs 97,600 fee saved me over Rs 60,000 compared to LPU or CU.
items.disliked: NIU brand is not recognised at large MNC HR departments the way Amity or NMIMS is. Career support for HR roles was limited.
items.body: For HR executives already in mid-career roles, NIU provides a solid credential upgrade at the lowest NAAC A+ fee. The 10 HR subjects cover both traditional IR and modern HR Analytics. Brand is a trade-off, but the UGC-DEB approval validates the degree for promotions.

items.name: Amit
items.liked: Industrial Relations and Labour Laws and Talent Management subjects were well-structured. Fee advantage was the main draw.
items.disliked: EMI interest rate was unclear at enrolment. Had to confirm separately with the financing partner. No campus connect events for HR networking.
items.body: Good HRM coverage for the price. The Leadership Power and Politics subject is a useful addition that most budget MBAs skip. Confirm EMI terms before enrolling. Self-sourced networking and job search are necessary.

items.name: Kavita
items.liked: Performance and Reward Management and Cross Cultural Management were practical. WES recognition helped with my overseas application.
items.disliked: No industry guest lectures or SHRM certification partnerships. Theory-heavy for some subjects.
items.body: At this price point, the HRM subject depth is strong. I completed the programme alongside my full-time HR role. The 10 subjects cover most areas an HR manager needs. Practical application depends on your own workplace exposure.

faqs.question: How many HR subjects does NIU Online MBA HRM cover?
faqs.answer: 10 dedicated HRM subjects across Sem 3-4, including Training and Development, Talent Management, IR and Labour Laws, Performance and Reward Management, HR Analytics, Strategic HRM, Cross Cultural Management, Negotiation and Persuasion, and Leadership Power and Politics.

faqs.question: Does NIU Online MBA HRM include HR Analytics?
faqs.answer: Yes. HR Analytics is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering workforce data analysis, attrition prediction, engagement measurement, and HR dashboard design. This adds a data-driven dimension to the traditional HRM curriculum.

faqs.question: Is NIU Online MBA valid for HRBP and HR Manager roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. The programme is UGC-DEB approved and the MBA degree is valid for all HR roles including HRBP, HR Manager, L&D Manager, and Talent Acquisition Manager positions across industries.

faqs.question: What is the total fee for NIU Online MBA HRM?
faqs.answer: Rs 97,600 all-in after the 25% Early Bird discount. This includes Rs 88,500 tuition, Rs 8,000 exam fees, and Rs 1,100 registration fee. No additional charges.

faqs.question: How does NIU HRM MBA compare to Amity HRM MBA?
faqs.answer: Amity is NIRF #22 with stronger brand recognition at Rs 2,25,000 (2.3x NIU's cost). NIU offers 10 dedicated HR subjects at Rs 97,600. Amity has a larger HR alumni network. Choose based on whether budget or brand is your priority.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A+ online MBA with 10 dedicated HRM subjects at Rs 97,600 all-in.
You are a Delhi-NCR HR professional who prioritises affordability over brand premium and NIRF ranking.
You want both traditional HR (IR, Labour Laws) and modern HR (HR Analytics, Strategic HRM) coverage at a sub-1-lakh price.
consider: If brand recognition at large MNC HR departments matters, Amity (Rs 2.25L, NIRF #22) or CU (Rs 1.65L, NIRF #19) are stronger choices.
If zero-cost EMI is essential, LPU offers guaranteed zero-cost 24-month EMI. NIU's EMI terms may carry interest.
If you want the cheapest government-backed MBA, IGNOU at Rs 66,000 is available, though with limited modern HR subject depth.
